title: The One Where Theyre Up All Night

jokes.text: Ross: Well, technically, it started 7 billion years ago.
jokes.setup: Someone asks when the comet starts
jokes.punchline: Ross gives an overly literal, pedantic answer about cosmic timescales
jokes.explanation: Classic Ross character moment - his pedantic nature creates humor through overthinking a simple question

jokes.text: RACHEL: No, no. JOEY: Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Ross gives an overly technical answer
jokes.punchline: Immediate frustrated reactions from friends who know Ross too well
jokes.explanation: Strong economy - two words each convey complete exasperation with Ross's behavior

jokes.text: Okay. Fine, I'll stop. No teaching, okay?
jokes.setup: Friends are clearly annoyed by his pedantry
jokes.punchline: Ross reluctantly agrees to stop 'teaching' - acknowledging his annoying habit
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Ross recognizes his tendency to lecture but frames it as 'teaching'

jokes.text: We'll just watch the pretty light streaking across the sky.
jokes.setup: Ross promised to stop being pedantic
jokes.punchline: He immediately becomes patronizingly simple instead
jokes.explanation: Smart escalation - Ross can't help himself and swings to the opposite extreme of baby talk

jokes.text: Whose official name is Bapstein-King. Okay! Okay!
jokes.setup: Ross was trying to be simple
jokes.punchline: He immediately reverts to pedantic mode before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - shows Ross literally cannot help himself, creates running gag with comet name

jokes.text: That's a plane. / Well, all right, 1700 bags of peanuts flying that high... that's pretty amazing too.
jokes.setup: Everyone is looking at what they think is the comet
jokes.punchline: It's just a plane, but Ross finds a way to make that seem profound too
jokes.explanation: Creative recovery joke - Ross turns embarrassment into wonder through absurd specificity of '1700 bags'

jokes.text: I wonder if you can see my apartment. / No, you can't. / What? / I don't know.
jokes.setup: Someone wonders if they can see their apartment from a plane
jokes.punchline: Immediate definitive 'no' followed by confusion about why they said it
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist moment - the randomness of the definitive answer followed by confusion

jokes.text: Know what else makes you wonder? Check out the rack on that chick.
jokes.setup: Philosophical moment about infinite space making you wonder
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately pivots to checking out women
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - takes profound moment and immediately makes it about sex, very character appropriate

jokes.text: I mean, Chandler's getting chilly.
jokes.setup: Complaint about waiting outside for comets
jokes.punchline: Blaming someone else for being cold as excuse to leave
jokes.explanation: Good setup for next joke - deflects personal discomfort onto Chandler

jokes.text: No, I'm not. / Then why are you wearing Monica's jacket? / Because it's flattering!
jokes.setup: Chandler denies being cold
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's wearing Monica's jacket because it's 'flattering' rather than for warmth
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler moment - vanity trumps masculine norms, unexpected and character-perfect answer

jokes.text: Shh. Get me out of here.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to join a couple who want to be alone
jokes.punchline: Awkward whispered plea for rescue from uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy - recognizing social awkwardness and wanting escape

jokes.text: Check this out. / Is it the comet? / No. Look, there's a bug stuck in tar right here.
jokes.setup: Ross is excited to finally see the comet they came for
jokes.punchline: It's just someone excited about a bug stuck in tar
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - builds Ross's hope then deflates it with mundane discovery

jokes.text: You know, there's two women, dude.
jokes.setup: Ross is frustrated that Joey only cares about women instead of the comet
jokes.punchline: Joey's defense is that there are TWO women, not just one
jokes.explanation: Joey logic - missing the point entirely and thinking quantity justifies his distraction

jokes.text: You wanted to come up here and look for some stupid Burger King comet.
jokes.setup: Joey is angry about being locked on the roof
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'Burger King comet' instead of Bapstein-King
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey malapropism - turns scientific name into fast food chain, very quotable

jokes.text: It's called the Bapstein-King comet, okay? Hey, Bapstein was a very well-respected astronomer from... / No! No! No!
jokes.setup: Joey mispronounces the comet name
jokes.punchline: Ross can't help but correct him even in crisis, gets shut down
jokes.explanation: Excellent character consistency - even trapped on a roof, Ross can't resist lecturing

jokes.text: Deep Impact was the one with Robert Duvall. Armageddon is what's going to happen to you if you wake me up.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks about which movie is which
jokes.punchline: Monica cleverly uses 'Armageddon' to threaten him for waking her
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay - uses movie title as threat, perfect economy and structure

jokes.text: Because every time I look at that cover, I'm like: [SNORES]
jokes.setup: Chandler describes a romance novel cover
jokes.punchline: Acts out falling asleep while describing it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy bit - acts out the boring effect of the book cover

jokes.text: It's in the living room, where there's also a light... and no one will kick you in the shin.
jokes.setup: Chandler wants to read in the bedroom
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals she kicked him and threatens to do it again
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - reveals previous violence and threatens more

jokes.text: Please don't be a spaceship. Please don't be a spaceship.
jokes.setup: Smoke alarm is beeping
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's first thought is that it might be aliens
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her mind goes to aliens first, very character-specific absurdity

jokes.text: How could you be beeping? I just disconnected you! I took out your battery! How can...? [BEEPS] Don't interrupt me!
jokes.setup: Phoebe is confused why the alarm still beeps
jokes.punchline: She treats the beeping as the alarm interrupting her rant
jokes.explanation: Great anthropomorphizing - Phoebe treats the alarm as rude for interrupting her

jokes.text: Ross. / I was thinking we'd just go down the fire escape.
jokes.setup: Ross and Joey are yelling for help from the roof
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests the obvious solution they overlooked
jokes.explanation: Good deflation joke - they were overthinking a simple problem

jokes.text: I know. I wasn't finished. / But don't worry! We're gonna go down the fire escape!
jokes.setup: Ross realizes Joey is right about the fire escape
jokes.punchline: He continues yelling to 'complete' his plea for help
jokes.explanation: Ross's compulsive need to finish what he started, even when pointless

jokes.text: With a wok?
jokes.setup: Chandler says he's making warm milk
jokes.punchline: Monica questions his choice of cookware
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - wrong tool for the job, economical delivery

jokes.text: Damn you, Oprah!
jokes.setup: Book that was supposed to be boring became interesting
jokes.punchline: Blames Oprah for making the book engaging through her book club
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference - Oprah's book club was known for making books popular

jokes.text: The second sister dies?!
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions second sister dying
jokes.punchline: Monica thinks he spoiled her book when he was talking about a different book
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up the Archie and Jughead punchline

jokes.text: The second sister dies in Archie and Jughead Double Digest? / That's correct.
jokes.setup: Monica realizes the book spoiler confusion
jokes.punchline: Chandler deadpan confirms a death in a wholesome comic book
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - treating wholesome comics as having dramatic deaths, Chandler's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Like the thing when you put the phone in your pants?
jokes.setup: Tag denies understanding Rachel's jokes
jokes.punchline: Rachel references his embarrassing habit of phone placement
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to earlier cringe moment, sets up running gag

jokes.text: Then I put a little heart in the corner because I didn't wanna seem too bossy.
jokes.setup: Rachel describes her serious work note
jokes.punchline: Reveals she added a heart to soften the demanding tone
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - shows Rachel trying to balance authority with likability

jokes.text: Maybe I'm embarrassed because you talk on the phone with your crotch.
jokes.setup: Tag suggests Rachel would be embarrassed if wrong
jokes.punchline: Rachel deflects by bringing up his embarrassing phone habit again
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of running gag, Rachel using it as defensive weapon

jokes.text: Oh! When did you unhook this? Nice work!
jokes.setup: Chandler and Monica are heading to the office
jokes.punchline: Chandler notices Monica's bra is undone and compliments his 'work'
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy moment - Chandler's pride in his bra-unhooking skills

jokes.text: It'll be just like bungee jumping, you know? But instead of bouncing back up, you won't.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to reassure Joey about jumping
jokes.punchline: His 'reassuring' comparison actually makes it sound worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - starts reassuring then becomes terrifying, great misdirection

jokes.text: Well, I'm not gonna lie to you, Joey. It's a possibility.
jokes.setup: Joey worries about smacking his head on concrete
jokes.punchline: Ross bluntly confirms this danger instead of reassuring
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Ross's honesty is unhelpful and makes situation worse

jokes.text: Tail. Oh! / Can you see what it is? / No.
jokes.setup: They flip a coin to decide who jumps
jokes.punchline: The coin lands somewhere they can't see it
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - their attempt at fairness backfires

jokes.text: What do you want from me?!
jokes.setup: Smoke alarm keeps beeping despite Phoebe's efforts
jokes.punchline: She yells at the alarm like it's a person making demands
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Phoebe's frustration, treating inanimate object as adversary

jokes.text: And who's this chippy? A little young for you, but whatever. / That's my sister. / Really? Very cute braces.
jokes.setup: Rachel finds a photo and makes assumptions
jokes.punchline: It's his sister, and Rachel doubles down with backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Rachel's inappropriate comment followed by awkward recovery

jokes.text: How can your genitals make phone calls? Okay? It's not a perfect world. Just go, please?
jokes.setup: Tag questions how contracts could be in copy room
jokes.punchline: Rachel uses his phone habit to argue anything's possible
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of running gag - Rachel uses it as logic for impossible things

jokes.text: I still don't get it.
jokes.setup: Entire phone-in-pants conversation
jokes.punchline: Tag still doesn't understand what Rachel finds funny about his habit
jokes.explanation: Good button to the running gag - Tag's obliviousness to his own oddness

jokes.text: You do know that was me that just said that, right?
jokes.setup: Monica asks if Chandler is still awake
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the logical impossibility of her question
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor - pointing out absurdity of common late-night conversation

jokes.text: 'Do you want soup or salad?' Both, always order both.
jokes.setup: Joey is hungry and regretting dinner choices
jokes.punchline: His life philosophy about always ordering both options
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his relationship with food and inability to choose

jokes.text: Noisy bitch!
jokes.setup: Phoebe has been fighting the smoke alarm all episode
jokes.punchline: She finally snaps and insults the alarm directly
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the smoke alarm storyline - Phoebe's breaking point

jokes.text: No, we were in the middle of sex... and you fell asleep. / No. No, that's not true. / No, best time ever. / Yeah. You rock my world.
jokes.setup: Monica fell asleep during sex
jokes.punchline: Monica's obviously fake denials get increasingly absurd
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Monica's transparent lies get more ridiculous

jokes.text: Well, I probably won't spill coffee grounds all over the kitchen floor. / Okay, I'm up. I'm up.
jokes.setup: Chandler threatens to make coffee to wake Monica
jokes.punchline: The threat of him making a mess immediately motivates Monica
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Monica's cleaning obsession used as motivation

jokes.text: So they slid out of your bottom drawer... crawled across the floor, then jumped onto my desk? / How did you know they were in my bottom drawer?
jokes.setup: Rachel denies putting contracts on Tag's desk
jokes.punchline: Tag's sarcastic response reveals Rachel knew exactly where they were
jokes.explanation: Excellent trap - Tag catches Rachel in her lie through her own specificity

jokes.text: I am so hot for you right now.
jokes.setup: Rachel has been caught in an embarrassing lie
jokes.punchline: She responds with sudden sexual attraction to Tag's cleverness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Rachel finds intelligence sexy, even when it exposes her

jokes.text: Your fire alarm was in the trash. / That's not mine. / Yes, it is. / How do you know? / If you dump a fire alarm, don't use a blanket that says: 'Property of Phoebe Buffay, not Monica.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe denies the fire alarm is hers
jokes.punchline: She wrapped it in a blanket with her name specifically saying it's not Monica's
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe moment - her attempt to frame Monica backfires spectacularly through over-specificity

jokes.text: Do you have a search warrant? Because the last time I checked, this was still America.
jokes.setup: Phoebe is caught red-handed with the fire alarm
jokes.punchline: She invokes constitutional rights against a fireman doing his job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - treating fire safety as government oppression, very quotable

jokes.text: There's a reset button? Oh, thank you. Thank you. / Oh. There's a reset button? My God! Why didn't I see that? Reset button. Reset button. Where is there a reset button? Oh, here it is! Oh! [CONTINUES BEEPING]
jokes.setup: Fireman tells her about reset button
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's relief turns to confusion when she can't make it work
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - false hope followed by continued frustration

jokes.text: Oh, my... How much do you weigh, Ross? / I prefer not to answer that right now. I'm still carrying a little holiday weight.
jokes.setup: Ross is climbing down Joey and crushing him
jokes.punchline: Ross deflects weight question with euphemistic excuse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Ross's vanity about weight even in crisis situation

jokes.text: Ross, my pants are starting to come down, and I'm not wearing any underwear. / Whoa!
jokes.setup: Joey and Ross are in compromising climbing position
jokes.punchline: Wardrobe malfunction creates even more awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy escalation - awkward situation becomes more awkward

jokes.text: Ow! My ankle! I really hurt my ankle! I think I twisted it when... Ooh, a quarter!
jokes.setup: Joey is in pain from falling and hurting his ankle
jokes.punchline: His attention immediately shifts to finding money on the ground
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment - his distractibility and love of finding money trumps pain

jokes.text: I have to be up in seven minutes. / Well, you're not gonna believe this, but if you have seven minutes... / Really? You want to? / Okay. You get the vacuum cleaner. I'll get the furniture polish.
jokes.setup: Chandler seems to be suggesting sex with seven minutes left
jokes.punchline: Monica thinks he's talking about cleaning instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work - Monica's cleaning obsession trumps sexual interpretation, perfect ending
